pr.title: Fix reorg data loss and RPC timer leak
pr.body:
Addresses two audit findings: expired names lost during blockchain reorganizations (HIGH-4) and accumulating auto-lock timers in RPC (MEDIUM-4).

## Expired name restoration during reorg

Blockchain reorgs across name expiration boundaries permanently deleted names. Added rollback support:

- **Storage**: `StoreExpiredName()` persists names to `expiredNamesBucket` indexed by expiration height before deletion
- **Restoration**: `RestoreExpiredNamesForBlock()` restores names when disconnecting blocks, maintaining both name records and expiration index
- **Cleanup**: `CleanupOldExpiredNames()` prunes old backups beyond reorg safety window
- **Integration**: `handleExpiredNames()` stores before deleting; `rollbackNameOperations()` restores as first step

Example rollback scenario:
```go
// Name expires at height 36101
handleExpiredNames(36101)  // Stores then deletes

// Reorg disconnects block 36101
rollbackNameOperations(block) // RestoreExpiredNamesForBlock(36101)
// Name is now active again with original ExpiresAt
```

## RPC timer cancellation

Multiple `walletpassphrase` calls created accumulating timers - earlier timers could lock wallet before expected timeout. 

- Added `autoLockTimer` field to `Server` with dedicated mutex
- Cancel existing timer before creating new one in `walletpassphrase`
- Cancel timer on manual `walletlock`
- Timer self-clears on expiration

**All tests pass with race detector enabled.**
pr.author: Copilot
